Facebook今天加入微软的行列，谴责苹果的App Store政策。这家社交媒体公司正在推出适用于iOS的Facebook游戏应用-主要是一款用于观看流媒体用户玩视频游戏的应用-但为了通过苹果App Store严格的审批程序，该公司不得不删除了该应用的小游戏功能。Facebook对这一妥协并不满意。
Facebook首席运营官谢丽尔·桑德伯格(Sheryl Sandberg)在给The Verge的一份新闻声明中表示：“不幸的是，为了获得苹果对独立Facebook游戏应用程序的批准，我们不得不完全取消游戏功能--这意味着iOS用户的体验不如使用Android的用户，”Facebook首席运营官谢丽尔·桑德伯格(Sheryl Sandberg)在给The Verge的一份新闻声明中表示。“我们将继续专注于为每月在Facebook上玩游戏的超过3.8亿人建立社区--无论苹果是否允许在独立的应用程序中这样做。”
Facebook表示，近几个月来，Facebook游戏应用程序多次被苹果拒绝。该公司表示，苹果引用了App Store准则4.7来证明拒绝的理由，声称Facebook游戏应用程序的主要目的是玩游戏。Facebook表示，它分享了Android Facebook游戏应用程序的使用数据，数据显示95%的活动都在观看流媒体，但这并没有改变苹果的立场。
早在6月份，苹果就在WWDC上公布了针对此类情况的上诉程序，但Facebook表示，它尝试了这一程序，但未能说服苹果推翻其决定。Facebook的一位发言人说：“我们甚至根据WWDC宣布的新的应用程序审查程序对该指导方针提出了上诉。”“我们没有收到回复。”
Facebook现在被迫放弃，并从今天在iOS上发布的独立应用程序中完全删除游戏。Facebook游戏应用程序主要用于观看游戏流，就像Twitch在iOS和Android上都使用一样。但在Android上，这款应用程序还包括Facebook即时游戏平台上的一些小游戏。这是苹果不会允许的。
这也不是Facebook第一次遇到App Store问题。Facebook游戏主管Vivek Sharma在给The Verge的一份声明中解释说：“即使是在Facebook的主要应用程序和Messenger上，我们多年来也被迫将即时游戏埋葬在iOS上。”“这是整个游戏行业的共同痛苦，这最终伤害了玩家和开发者，并严重阻碍了移动设备上对其他类型格式的创新，比如云游戏。”
苹果正面临来自竞争对手和游戏行业越来越多的批评，要求其放松App Store的限制。本周早些时候，微软被迫削减了XCloud iOS的测试，因为App Store的政策几个月来一直阻止该公司推出这款应用。微软昨日做出不同寻常的决定，谴责苹果，称“苹果是唯一拒绝消费者使用云游戏和Xbox Game Pass等游戏订阅服务的通用平台。”
苹果为其在App Store中屏蔽XCloud、Stadia和GeForce等云游戏服务的决定进行了辩护。苹果发言人昨天在一份声明中表示：“我们的客户享受着数百万开发者提供的优秀应用和游戏，只要他们遵循适用于所有开发者的同一套指导方针，包括单独提交游戏供审查，并出现在图表和搜索中，游戏服务就完全可以在App Store上推出。”
苹果的理由是，该公司不能单独审查流媒体服务上提供的游戏，但该公司似乎不会在Netflix或YouTube等服务上遇到同样的问题，这些服务将数百万视频、电视节目和电影流式传输到苹果的iOS设备上，而苹果不可能对这些服务进行审查。
苹果最新的软弱的App Store借口是在该公司卷入新的嘿电子邮件应用程序的激烈斗争几个月后。在最初的拒绝招致立法者和开发者的广泛谴责后，苹果最终批准了这款应用。苹果是目前面临潜在反垄断行动的多家美国科技公司中的一员。欧盟还对苹果App Store和Apple Pay的做法展开了正式的反垄断调查。随着微软和Facebook公开批评苹果，该公司在接下来的几个月里肯定会面临更多关于其App Store政策的质疑。
note_en:
Facebook is joining Microsoft in condemning Apple’s App Store policies today. The social media company is launching its Facebook Gaming app for iOS — primarily an app used to watch streamers play video games — but has had to remove the app’s mini games feature to pass Apple’s strict App Store approval process. Facebook isn’t happy about the compromise.
 “Unfortunately, we had to remove gameplay functionality entirely in order to get Apple’s approval on the standalone Facebook Gaming app — meaning iOS users have an inferior experience to those using Android,” said Sheryl Sandberg, Facebook’s chief operating officer in a press statement given to  The Verge. “We’re staying focused on building communities for the more than 380 million people who play games on Facebook every month — whether Apple allows it in a standalone app or not.”
 Facebook says it has had the  Facebook Gaming app rejected multiple times by Apple in recent months. The company says Apple has cited  App Store guideline 4.7 to justify the rejections, claiming the primary purpose of the Facebook Gaming app is to play games. Facebook says it shared usage data from its Android Facebook Gaming app that showed 95 percent of activity is watching streams, but this didn’t change Apple’s stance.
   Apple unveiled an appeal process for situations like this at WWDC back in June, but Facebook says it tried this and failed to convince Apple to overturn its decision. “We even appealed the guideline under the new app review process announced at WWDC,” says a Facebook spokesperson. “We did not receive a response.”
 Facebook has now been forced to give up and remove games entirely from the standalone app launching on iOS today. The Facebook Gaming app is primarily used to watch streams of games, much like Twitch is used on both iOS and Android. But on Android, the app also includes a number of mini games from Facebook’s Instant Games platform. That’s what Apple won’t allow.
 This isn’t the first time that Facebook has run into App Store issues, either. “Even on the main Facebook app and Messenger, we’ve been forced to bury Instant Games for years on iOS,” explains Facebook Gaming chief Vivek Sharma in a statement to  The Verge. “This is shared pain across the games industry, which ultimately hurts players and devs and severely hamstrings innovation on mobile for other types of formats, like cloud gaming.”
   Apple is facing growing criticism from rivals and the gaming industry to loosen its App Store restrictions. Microsoft was  forced to cut its xCloud iOS testing earlier this week, after App Store policies have been preventing the company from launching the app for months. Microsoft took the  unusual decision to condemn Apple yesterday, saying “Apple stands alone as the only general purpose platform to deny consumers from cloud gaming and game subscription services like Xbox Game Pass.”
  Apple has defended its decision to block cloud gaming services like xCloud, Stadia, and GeForce Now from the App Store. “Our customers enjoy great apps and games from millions of developers, and gaming services can absolutely launch on the App Store as long as they follow the same set of guidelines applicable to all developers, including submitting games individually for review, and appearing in charts and search,” said an Apple spokesperson in a statement yesterday.
 Apple’s argument is that the company can’t individually review games offered on streaming services, but the company doesn’t seem to have the same issues with services like Netflix or YouTube that stream millions of videos, TV shows, and movies to Apple’s iOS devices that Apple can’t possibly review.
 Apple’s latest weak App Store excuse comes just months after the company was embroiled in a  bitter battle over the new Hey email app. Apple eventually approved the app after the initial rejection drew widespread condemnation from lawmakers and developers. Apple is part of a number of US tech companies currently facing  potential antitrust action. The EU has also  opened up a formal antitrust investigation into Apple’s App Store and Apple Pay practices. With Microsoft and Facebook openly criticizing Apple, the company is bound to face even more questions over its App Store policies in the months ahead.
title: Facebook抨击苹果的App Store政策，在iOS上推出Facebook游戏，但没有应用程序的小游戏功能，以通过App Store的严格审批政策
title_en: Facebook slams Apple's App Store policies, launches Facebook Gaming on iOS without the app's mini games feature to pass the App Store's strict approval policies

TikTok威胁要就唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)总统禁止美国公司和个人与母公司ByteDance做生意的行政命令起诉特朗普政府。
特朗普周四签署了这项行政命令，辩称中国公司构成国家安全风险。
TikTok在周五发布的一份声明中表示：我们对最近的行政命令感到震惊，该命令在没有任何正当程序的情况下发布。
近一年来，我们一直寻求真诚地与美国政府接触，为表达的关切提供建设性的解决方案，它补充道。相反，我们遇到的是政府无视事实，在没有经过标准法律程序的情况下口述协议条款，并试图将自己插入私营企业之间的谈判。
TikTok表示，这项将于9月生效的行政命令是非法的，它可能会在法庭上挑战指令。
该公司表示，我们将寻求所有可用的补救措施，以确保法治不会被抛弃，确保我们的公司和我们的用户得到公平对待-如果不是政府，那就是美国法院。
本周有报道称，特朗普政府试图迫使TikTok出售其美国业务，否则将面临禁令。微软已经公开宣布，它正在就TikTok在美国、加拿大、澳大利亚和新西兰的业务进行收购谈判。周四，特朗普也发布了类似的行政命令，目标是腾讯拥有的微信。
TikTok指责特朗普政府无视正当程序，藐视法律。这项行政命令可能会破坏全球企业对美国的信任，它表示，法治一直是吸引投资的磁石，并刺激了美国数十年的经济增长。
TikTok是一个充满创意和激情的社区，一个为家庭带来欢乐、为创作者带来有意义职业的家园。我们正在为长期建设这个平台。TikTok在未来的许多年里都会在这里。
我们对最近的行政命令感到震惊，该命令是在没有任何正当程序的情况下发布的。近一年来，我们一直寻求与美国政府真诚接触，为表达的关切提供建设性的解决方案。我们所遇到的，是政府漠视事实，没有经过正规的法律程序，便口授协议条款，企图插手私人企业之间的谈判。
我们明确表示，我们打算与适当的官员合作，设计一种解决方案，使我们的用户、创作者、合作伙伴、员工和更广泛的美国社区受益。过去没有，现在仍然没有正当程序，也没有遵守法律。该决定的文本清楚地表明，人们一直依赖未具名、没有引用的报道，担心这款应用可能被用于虚假信息宣传，而这种担忧没有得到证实，以及对收集数据的担忧，这是全球数千款移动应用的行业标准。我们已经明确表示，TikTok从未与中国政府共享用户数据，也从未应中国政府的要求审查内容。事实上，我们在我们的透明中心提供了我们的审核指导方针和算法源代码，这是任何同行公司都没有承诺的问责级别。我们甚至表示愿意寻求将美国业务全部出售给一家美国公司。
这项行政命令可能会破坏全球企业对美国法治的信任，法治一直是吸引投资的磁石，并刺激了美国数十年的经济增长。它还为言论自由和开放市场的概念树立了一个危险的先例。我们将寻求所有可用的补救措施，以确保法治不会被抛弃，确保我们的公司和我们的用户得到公平对待-如果不是政府，那就是美国法院。
我们希望喜欢我们平台的1亿美国人知道：TikTok对你们的承诺从未动摇过，也永远不会动摇，因为它是你们表达、娱乐和联系的家园。我们始终将您的安全、安保和社区的信任放在首位。作为TikTok的用户、创建者、合作伙伴和家人，您有权向您选出的代表(包括白宫)表达您的意见。你有权发表意见。

note_en:
TikTok has threatened to sue the Trump administration over President Donald Trump's executive order barring US companies and individuals from doing business with its parent company ByteDance.
 Trump  signed the executive order Thursday, arguing that the Chinese firm poses a national security risk.
 "We are shocked by the recent Executive Order, which was issued without any due process," TikTok said in a  statement published Friday.
 "For nearly a year, we have sought to engage with the US government in good faith to provide a constructive solution to the concerns that have been expressed," it added. "What we encountered instead was that the Administration paid no attention to facts, dictated terms of an agreement without going through standard legal processes, and tried to insert itself into negotiations between private businesses."
   TikTok suggested that the executive order — set to take effect in September — was illegal and that it might challenge the diktat in court.
 "We will pursue all remedies available to us in order to ensure that the rule of law is not discarded and that our company and our users are treated fairly — if not by the Administration, then by the US courts," the company said.
 This week reports emerged that the Trump administration was trying to force TikTok to sell off its US business or face a ban.  Microsoft has publicly announced it's pursuing acquisition talks for TikTok's US, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand businesses. Trump also issued a similar executive order on Thursday targeting the Tencent-owned WeChat.
 TikTok accused the Trump administration of flouting the law by ignoring due process. "This Executive Order risks undermining global businesses' trust in the United States' commitment to the rule of law, which has served as a magnet for investment and spurred decades of American economic growth," it said.
    "TikTok is a community full of creativity and passion, a home that brings joy to families and meaningful careers to creators. And we are building this platform for the long term. TikTok will be here for many years to come.
 "We are shocked by the recent Executive Order, which was issued without any due process. For nearly a year, we have sought to engage with the US government in good faith to provide a constructive solution to the concerns that have been expressed. What we encountered instead was that the Administration paid no attention to facts, dictated terms of an agreement without going through standard legal processes, and tried to insert itself into negotiations between private businesses.
 "We made clear our intentions to work with the appropriate officials to devise a solution to benefit our users, creators, partners, employees, and the broader community in the United States. There has been, and continues to be, no due process or adherence to the law. The text of the decision makes it plain that there has been a reliance on unnamed "reports" with no citations, fears that the app "may be" used for misinformation campaigns with no substantiation of such fears, and concerns about the collection of data that is industry standard for thousands of mobile apps around the world. We have made clear that TikTok has never shared user data with the Chinese government, nor censored content at its request. In fact, we make our moderation guidelines and algorithm source code available in our Transparency Center, which is a level of accountability no peer company has committed to. We even expressed our willingness to pursue a full sale of the US business to an American company.
 "This Executive Order risks undermining global businesses' trust in the United States' commitment to the rule of law, which has served as a magnet for investment and spurred decades of American economic growth. And it sets a dangerous precedent for the concept of free expression and open markets. We will pursue all remedies available to us in order to ensure that the rule of law is not discarded and that our company and our users are treated fairly – if not by the Administration, then by the US courts.
   "We want the 100 million Americans who love our platform because it is your home for expression, entertainment, and connection to know: TikTok has never, and will never, waver in our commitment to you. We prioritize your safety, security, and the trust of our community – always. As TikTok users, creators, partners, and family, you have the right to express your opinions to your elected representatives, including the White House. You have the right to be heard."

note:
周五，美国总统特朗普签署了一项行政命令，禁止美国实体与腾讯及其广受欢迎的社交媒体应用微信进行交易，几个小时后，腾讯控股的股价暴跌了10%。
腾讯在香港上市的股票在开盘时开始暴跌，收盘时下跌了5%，收复了早先的失地。腾讯是其主要成分股的基准恒生指数下跌1.6%。
就在消息公布前，腾讯股价接近纪录高位，该公司市值已接近Facebook。
周四晚上，特朗普在华盛顿发布了两项行政命令，将对微信和TikTok施加限制，加剧了与北京的紧张关系。这些命令禁止在美国的人或受美国司法管辖的个人与这两款应用程序的中国所有者进行交易，从8月6日起45天生效。
微信的命令没有详细说明哪些交易会受到影响，但影响可能是广泛的。这款颇受欢迎的社交媒体应用程序用于短信、移动支付和数十项其他功能，在中国大陆和其他地方拥有超过12亿用户。
微信也被美国和其他国家的人下载。许多在中国有业务的美国公司目前利用其广泛的社交媒体平台向这个世界上人口最多的国家的消费者推销他们的产品和服务。
总部位于深圳的腾讯也是世界上最大的视频游戏公司之一，其游戏被世界各地的人玩。
跟踪腾讯的分析师周五争先恐后地计算出该公司有多少业务可能受到影响。
“如果制裁只针对微信，而不是全面打击腾讯在美国的业务，对腾讯的影响将非常有限，”香港海通国际证券分析师梁比利说。
上周五之前，腾讯的股价一直在飙升，今年到目前为止上涨了48%。截至周四，该公司市值为6,870亿美元，是中国市值第二高的上市公司，仅次于阿里巴巴集团控股的阿里巴巴香港上市股票周五下跌3%。
在冠状病毒大流行期间，人们花更多的时间在家里和网上，与朋友聊天，玩视频游戏，使用其应用程序进行更多的在线购物，腾讯一直是受益者之一。
周五，亚洲各地股指涨跌互现。上证综合指数下跌了1%，日本日经225指数下跌了0.4%，而韩国综合股价指数上涨了0.4%。
霸菱驻香港大中华区投资主管Khiem Do表示，“中美地缘政治紧张局势升级正在打压人气”，并打压科技股.。他表示，亚洲许多科技股最近股价的强劲上涨，也可能使它们更容易受到抛售的影响。
新加坡里德·史密斯律师事务所(Reed Smith LLP)专门研究信息技术、隐私和数据安全的律师查米安·奥(Charmian Aw)表示，由于行政命令只引用了微信，腾讯科技帝国的其他部分不太可能受到影响。
“但我们将需要等待该命令涵盖的交易的确切细节发布，”她说。
华侨银行投资研究负责人卡门·李表示，除了TikTok之外，特朗普政府还针对微信，这表明美国和中国之间的冲突现在正从硬件转向软件技术。
“中国互联网公司可能会继续面临美国的监管压力，”她说，并补充说，更严格的监管可能会阻碍中国科技巨头的全球增长雄心。
note_en:
Shares of  Tencent Holdings    TCEHY  -5.96%  plunged as much as 10% on Friday, hours after President Trump  signed an executive order that would bar U.S. entities from transacting with the Chinese internet giant and its popular social-media app, WeChat.
  Tencent’s shares, which are listed in Hong Kong, started tumbling when the market opened and ended the day down 5% after paring earlier losses. The benchmark Hang Seng Index, which Tencent is a large constituent of, fell 1.6%.
  Just before the news, Tencent shares were trading near a record high and the company’s market capitalization had been close to that of  Facebook
    On Thursday evening in Washington, Mr. Trump issued a pair of executive orders that would impose limits on WeChat as well as TikTok, escalating tensions with Beijing. The orders bar people in the U.S. or individuals who are subject to U.S. jurisdiction from transactions with the China-based owners of both apps, effective 45 days from Aug. 6.
      The order on WeChat didn’t detail what kind of transactions would be affected, but the ramifications could be wide-ranging. The popular social-media app, which is used for messaging, mobile payments and scores of other functions, has more than 1.2 billion users in mainland China and elsewhere.
  WeChat has also been downloaded by people in the U.S. and other countries. And many American companies with businesses in China currently use its wide-reaching social media platform to market their products and services to consumers in the world’s most populous nation.
  Shenzhen-based Tencent is also one of the world’s largest videogame companies and its games are played by people world-wide.
  Analysts who follow Tencent were scrambling Friday to figure out how much of the company’s business could be affected.
  “The impact on Tencent will be very limited if the sanction is targeted only at WeChat, rather than a full-blown attack against Tencent’s business in the States,” said Billy Leung, an analyst with  Haitong International Securities  in Hong Kong.
  Before Friday, Tencent shares had been on a tear, gaining 48% in the year to date period. As of Thursday, the company had a market capitalization of $687 billion, making it China’s second-most valuable listed company after  Alibaba Group Holding  Alibaba’s Hong Kong-listed shares fell 3% on Friday.
  Tencent has been a beneficiary of people spending more time at home and online during the coronavirus pandemic, chatting with friends, playing videogames and making more purchases online using its apps.
  Stock indexes across Asia were mixed on Friday. The Shanghai Composite Index fell 1% and Japan’s Nikkei 225 lost 0.4%, though South Korea’s Kospi gained 0.4%.
    “The escalating geopolitical tension between China and the U.S. is weighing on sentiment” and on technology stocks, said Khiem Do, head of greater China investments at Barings in Hong Kong. He said a recent strong run-up in the stock prices of many technology stocks in Asia could also make them more vulnerable to a selloff.
  Charmian Aw, a lawyer who specializes in information technology, privacy and data security at Reed Smith LLP in Singapore, said since the executive order only cites WeChat, it is unlikely that other parts of Tencent’s tech empire will be affected.
  “But we will need to wait for the exact details of transactions covered by the order to be issued,” she said.
  The Trump administration’s targeting of WeChat—in addition to TikTok—shows how the conflict between the U.S. and China is now shifting to software technology from hardware, said Carmen Lee, head of OCBC Investment Research.
  “Chinese internet firms are likely to continue facing U.S. regulatory pressure,” she said, adding that more stringent regulation could crimp the global growth ambitions of China’s tech giants.
title: 周四市值6,870亿美元的腾讯股票，在特朗普对微信禁赛后暴跌一成以上。
title_en: Tencent stock, which had a market capitalization of $687B on Thursday, plunged 10%+ after Trump's ban on WeChat
